title: Trends & Insights
concept: The payoff of analytics: spotting trends, reading patterns, and turning them into decisions — while never mistaking correlation for causation.

exercises.prompt: Recap: best chart for a value changing over time?
exercises.explain: Right — line charts show trends over time.

cards.title: From charts to decisions
cards.body: Analytics only matters if it changes what you do. That payoff is called an insight.

cards.title: Trends
cards.body: A consistent direction over time: sales rising, users dropping, searches for "gifts" spiking every December.

cards.title: Correlation
cards.body: When two things move together — ice cream sales and sunburns both rise in summer. Useful to spot, but handle with care…

cards.title: Correlation ≠ causation
cards.body: Two things moving together doesn't mean one causes the other. A hidden cause (summer heat) can drive both. This is the #1 analytics mistake.

cards.title: Turning insight into action
cards.body: "Sales spike Friday evenings → run Friday promos." "Users quit at step 3 → fix step 3." Insight you act on.

options.text: A consistent pattern or direction over time

exercises.explain: A trend is a steady direction over time — rising, falling, or seasonal.

exercises.prompt: True or false: if two things rise together, one must be causing the other.
exercises.explain: False — correlation is not causation. A hidden third factor often explains both.

exercises.prompt: Ice cream sales and sunburns both rise in summer. Best explanation?

options.text: A hidden cause — summer heat — drives both

exercises.explain: Classic correlation-not-causation: a third factor (hot weather) drives both.

exercises.sentence: Two things moving together is correlation; it does not prove ___.
exercises.explain: Correlation ≠ causation — moving together doesn't prove one causes the other.

exercises.prompt: A store finds sales jump every Friday evening. Best move?

options.text: Act on the trend — run promotions Friday evenings
Assume Fridays magically create demand and do nothing

exercises.explain: Spot the trend → turn it into action. That's analytics paying off — the bridge to making AI-powered decisions.
